title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 9 October 2024.

items.title: **Hotel Barcelona** has been delayed to next year
bullets: The collaboration between Swery65 and Suda51 is now targeting "early 2025"

items.title: **Red Dead Redemption** and Undead Nightmare announced for PC
bullets: The open world western game will be bundled with its zombie-themed expansion Undead Nightmare when it hits PC on October 29, 2024.
The title will be available via the Rockstar Store, Steam and the Epic Games Store.

Port is created by Double Eleven and features " native 4K resolution at up to 144hz, ultrawide and super ultrawide support, HDR10 support, fully Keyboard and Mouse support, DLSS 3.7 and FSR 3 support"

items.title: **LittleBigPlanet 3** and LBP DLC are being delisted from the PlayStation Store
bullets: The PS4 platformer and the entire range of DLC for the franchise will be pulled from sale on October 31, 2024

items.title: **Saudi Arabia** has reduced its stake in Nintendo
bullets: The follows (later corrected) reports that it was planning to invest further
Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund (PIF) has cut its stake in Nintendo from 8.58% to 7.54%, a move which still makes it one of Nintendo’s largest shareholders

items.title: NCsoft Amazon Games' **Throne and Liberty** tops 3m players in launch week
**Tequilla Works** lays off "small number" of staff, cancels unannounced game
bullets: Rime and Sexy Brutale developer is restructuring the studio around a single project

items.title: **Humble Games** calls in Good Games Group for support following restructure
bullets: Humble Games is bringing in another third-party to support its operations following a restructuring that saw the entire staff laid off.

The company announced today that it will be working with Good Games Group, a new indie publisher set up by former Humble Games leaders Alan Patmore and Mark Nash, to "further support previously released titles."
This is in addition to the partnership with The Powell Group, which took over publishing operations after the layoffs.
